extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the sprint after skipping between leadout trains in final 5km rather than following his own Bora team's setup
tactical_patterns: Bora's leadout for Bauhaus failed as Bennett ignored his own team's train to follow bigger squads
Trek-Segafredo positioned Mosschetti through turns 1.3km out but couldn't hold position through final left-hander under 500m
Groenewegen led out own sprint from 300m with Jumbo-Visma, got boxed finishing 7th—team didn't control
Weather-induced crashes 15-20 minutes before finish caused splits; gap-closing efforts cost positioning in finale

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: tactically savvy ignoring own leadout to poach from rivals; first WorldTour win in new kit signals immediate comfort
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: finished second despite perfect Bora leadout positioning him; failed to convert team's work
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: forced into self-leadout from 300m with Jumbo-Visma, got boxed—team offered no protection
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Mike: positive: Trek positioned him through technical turns 1.3km out showing classics-style navigation in bunch sprint
